Sheet1.Subject: Dummy Subject
Sheet1.Node Description: Student works with numbers up to 9999 and performs four basic arithmetic operations
Sheet1.Evidence Description: Subtract numbers upto 4 digits and solve word problem
Sheet1.Question Stem:
A school library had 2,345 books at the beginning of the year. If 567 books were borrowed and not yet returned.


Drag and arrange the steps to find the number of books remaining in the library?
Sheet1.Step 1: Number of books at the beginning = 2345
Sheet1.Step 3: Number of books remaining = (Number of books at the beginning) – (Number of books borrowed)
Sheet1.Distractor Step 1: Number of books remaining = (Number of books at the beginning) + (Number of books borrowed)

Sheet1.Subject: Dummy Subject
Sheet1.Node Description: Student works with numbers up to 9999 and performs four basic arithmetic operations
Sheet1.Evidence Description: Multiply numbers upto 3 digit numbers and solve word problem
Sheet1.Question Stem:
A library has 120 shelves, and each shelf holds 9 books.


Drag and arrange the steps to find the total number of books that will be placed in all the shelves.
Sheet1.Step 1: Number of shelves in the library = 120
Sheet1.Step 2: Number of books each shelf can hold = 9
Sheet1.Step 3: The total number of books that will be placed in all the shelves = (Number of shelves in the library) × (Number of books each shelf can hold)
Sheet1.Step 4: The total number of books that will be placed in all the shelves = 120 × 9
Sheet1.Step 5: The total number of books that will be placed in all the shelves = 1080
Sheet1.Distractor Step 1: The total number of books that will be placed in all the shelves = (Number of shelves in the library) + (Number of books each shelf can hold)
Sheet1.Distractor Step 2: The total number of books that will be placed in all the shelves = 120 + 9
Sheet1.Distractor Step 3: The total number of books that will be placed in all the shelves = 129
